raw_consumer_statement:
My name is XXXX XXXX XXXX. My XXXX  XXXX and I are XXXX  cardholders who were victims of a sophisticated phone scam on XX/XX/year>. The scammers posed as XXXX agents and claimed to book us a vacation to XXXX XXXX. They charged our XXXX Citi credit card nearly {$15000.00} : Airfare  : {$7200.00} ( Ref. No. XXXX ) Lodging : {$7600.00} ( Ref. No. XXXX ) We never received any confirmations, tickets, or itineraries. No services were ever provided, and no real booking ever existed. 



On XX/XX/year>, while the charges were still pending, we immediately called Citi to report the fraud. XXXX independent witnesses, XXXX XXXX and XXXX XXXX, were present and participated on the XXXX way phone call. The Citi representative acknowledged that the charges could not be stopped while pending but assured us that we would not be held responsible since this was clearly fraud and a theft of service. Citi initially reversed the charges. 



Since then, Citi has sent several letters demanding we provide documentation of canceled services. This documentation does not exist because the trip was never real. We were scammed, and there was nothing to cancel. We were told at the time that we would not be liable, and we relied on that representation in good faith. 



Despite this, Citi reversed the refund, denied our dispute, and is now holding us responsible. This is unacceptable. There was no contract, no itinerary, and no travel. We reported the fraud immediately. Citi also failed to issue fraud alerts for these unusually large back-to-back charges, despite flagging much smaller transactions in the past. 



This is a clear case of XXXX  financial exploitation and theft of service, and Citi has failed in its obligations under the Fair Credit Billing Act to protect its customers.
